title: Yb scan descriptor
description: JSON descriptor for a single scan submission. Consumed by the MATLAB dispatch_descriptor.m on the MATLAB side and by yb_analysis/scans/client.py on the lab-PC side. Schema version 1.

schema_version.description: Descriptor schema version. Bump when adding incompatible features.

seq.description: Name of the MATLAB sequence function (e.g. 'CoolingSeq'), the literal string 'auto' to let dispatch_descriptor.m pick the seq from params (e.g. n_rounds branching in SLMRearrangement), or a function-handle object {'@': 'RearrangeCommSeq2'}.

params.description: Map of dotted ScanGroup paths -> scalar value OR sweep specification. Path components are field names (e.g. 'Cooling.Detuning'). A value may be a JSON scalar, array (vector), JSON-string array for cell-of-string MATLAB values, or a sweep object with 'scan'.

runp.description: Map of dotted runp() paths -> value. Same value shapes as 'params'.

opts.description: Extra varargin passed through to ybBuildScanPayload (and via that to ybStartScan when invoked directly). Each entry is a {key, value} pair preserved in order.

label.description: Optional human label for queue UI; falls back to seq name.

description.description: Optional free-text PURPOSE/CONTEXT for the run (NOT the name): why it was run, which test group it belongs to, what it measures. Stamped into the scan-config sidecar (top-level 'description') so the analysis dashboard shows it (collapsible) and runs are searchable by it. Inert for the byte/seq path.

code_snapshot.description: Optional reproducibility pin (#3): replay the experiment code (YbSeqs/YbSteps/YbScans) captured for a prior run instead of the live tree. Set by 're-queue (orig code)'. Inert for normal runs; honored by the pyctrl run loop, ignored by the MATLAB backend.

scan_id.description: 14-digit YYYYMMDDHHMMSS of the source run whose code snapshot to replay.

data_root.description: Optional override for the data root holding _code_snapshots (defaults to the scan-prep data root).

ParamValue.description: Either a scalar value, a vector value, a cell-of-string value, a function-handle value, or a sweep object.

logspace.description: [10^start, 10^stop, n] - inclusive logspace (MATLAB convention: arguments are exponents).

values.description: Explicit list of values. Items must be numeric (vector sweep) or strings (cell-of-string sweep).
